paragraphs.body: "Please Help!" are the words I heard coming from the bedroom as I prepared a nice bowl of Cocoa-Pebbles. I put down the jug of whole milk without the cap on, a singular fly waiting for me to leave the room as I hurried into the master bedroom. What I was looking at was the last thing I expected: my mom's entire hand shoved in my dad's mouth. Thick, dark red Blood oozed from my dad's mouth and onto my mom's hand, telling me to call the ambulance. Running into the kitchen, I grabbed my phone and dialed 9-9-1. The operator answered the phone in a dull, monotone voice, saying, "991. What's your emergency" I anxiously told them; my dad needs help, and he is bleeding out of his mouth. As I told the operator my address, she assured me everything would be ok and told me they had an ambulance in route. After hearing this, I nervously replied thank you for hanging up the phone. Running into the master bedroom, the same scene presents itself before me with the addition of barking and growling coming from my dogs on the bed, my dad unresponsive, and my mom bawling her eyes out, saying, "Everything is going to be ok." At this moment, I did not know if she was talking to him or reassuring herself. I asked my mother, "What is happening to Dad?" She responded with fear, screaming, "he is having a seizure." I tried to help my mother by handing her a towel to put in my dad's mouth so he wouldn't see through his tongue with his serrated teeth. Soon I hear, piercing Sirens surrounded the house, and I ran outside, telling them to come quickly. The brave men came inside my house, leaving the stretcher in the living room as I stood by the door, frightened by the scene. The men come out of the bedroom struggling, carrying my father in their hands as he squirms in their arms from the seizure. The men get him on the stretcher, strap him down, restricting his movement, and move him into the ambulance. As they sped out of the driveway, every individual leaf around the ambulance spiraled into the air, leaving what was once an ambulance with my father in it to an empty driveway with scattered leaves. My mother and I stare at the driveway and its emptiness, the only sound being the crackle of the leaves blown by the calm wind. When heading back inside, I asked my mother, "Why was Dad having a seizure. She replied with a trembling voice, "Your father is an addict." Without Saying another word, she burst into tears, burying her face into my shoulders. At this moment, I left as if I were a leaf that had fallen from its tree. Not only is my dad on his way to the emergency room, but the reason why has been kept a secret from me for who knows how long.

paragraphs.body: When my dad was being hauled off in a stretcher, it was a a surprisingly calm night outside. Besides the five dogs being contained behind the door, barking as if someone was killing them; The ambulance left the driveway and there was nothing except a brisk breeze blowing across my face, as I fought off tears. The huge tree towering over my house, that is most definitely a safety hazard, looked like something strait out of a horror film at a cemetery scene. No leaves, not even brown leaves that stay on a tree right before it dies. It was completely empty, only branches left hanging on for dear life trying not to break off from the random gusts of wind that would strike. Russet leaves and scattered acorns covered the driveway, except a small square where the ambulance screeched its tires when leaving. The only sound being the whistling wind as it blows across my face, and the faint sniffles coming from my mom, as she tries to stop her tears from streaking down her face.

annotations.text: Russet leaves and scattered acorns covered the driveway, except a small square where the ambulance screeched its tires when leaving.

annotations.comment: The descriptions of bleakness in this paragraph are striking. The tone they create is powerful and meaningful, especially for the purposes of your essay. Nice job.
annotations.context_left: killing them; The ambulance left the driveway and there was nothing except a brisk breeze

paragraphs.body: Being presented with this information in this way was hard. My thoughts were all over the place, I did not know what to think. The day after the only thoughts that came to mind were why didn’t my mom tell me that my dad was struggling with addiction? How did I not know? How could I live in the same room, see him every day and not know? The answer to my question was that I did not care to know. All of the days when he would stay in the master bedroom and not come out should have been a sign to me, and it wasn’t. I thought nothing of his strangely odd actions, because I was not paying attention. To me these actions were like a leaf lying on the ground. You might see it, but even if you step on it, you will not think anything of it. The answer that I still don't have is why did my mom not tell me? It is not like I was super young and there was no point in telling me. I was 15 years old, and my mother did not see it in her best interest to tell me. Having another person in the household, helping and holding someone accountable for their addiction could only help.

annotations.text: The day after the only thoughts that came to mind were why didn’t my mom tell me that my dad was struggling with addiction? How did I not know? How could I live in the same room, see him every day and not know? The answer to my question was that I did not care to know.
annotations.comment: This is such a great use of questions. They speak directly to your audience but in subtle and nuanced ways.

paragraphs.body: A leaf is more than an indication of the seasons changing. A leaf can resemble change in time, when we do not realize time is changing, or just a decoration on a tree making it pretty. A leaf does not have to be large and vibrant. A leaf does not have to even hang from a tree to be important. It can be brown and laying on the ground waiting to get stepped on and crumbled into a hundred little pieces, or it can be healthy and green blowing in the cool breeze. A leaf does so much more than providing shade to all the animals who call Earth home. A leaf shows us a continuation of time, when we do not realize time is passing at all. We only notice leaves when they are these nice vibrant and pretty colors, but what about when they are not. For around 6 months leaves do not come to your mind or attention, you do not notice these portraits of life, until they are begging you to look. Soon after you lay your eyes on the beautiful piece of art that is a leaf, it will fall from its tree and never change back into what it once was. A leaf is like many things in our day-to-day life that we take advantage of. We go on in our life's as normal not paying attention to the little changes, that build up to the leaves becoming the beautiful colors that we gaze upon and take pictures of.

annotations.comment: What is the metaphor of the leaf? What does it represent for you in terms of this essay and its central focusing idea?

paragraphs.body: Take in your life; Is the best advice I have. Do not live your life overlooking details like a dead leaf laying on the ground. especially when they revolve around your friends and family. What happened to me in my situation can be viewed as not being my fault. But if I had noticed the signs and pieced the details together, maybe I could have prevented this whole situation from happening.
